current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Run a proxy gated K=32 stabilization pass with AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 and EXPANSION_FACTOR=8 to test for a better low-K cost-quality tradeoff.

Run the first real proxy comparison for `group_topk` at K=128 using the current strongest gated training regime, but without the unsupported `GROUP_TOPK_SIZE` env override that caused the earlier crashes.

Run a proxy stabilization pass on the best gated K=128 EF=16 configuration with AUXK disabled to test whether pure reconstruction training can improve the current anchor without changing the core regime.

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: The last round showed the gated low-K capacity cut was a real quality regression, and the search has now gone several rounds without advancing an incubating alternative family. The least-bad informative move is to get a real proxy read on the already-implemented `group_topk` family at K=128 under the strongest current training regime, removing the unsupported `GROUP_TOPK_SIZE` override that caused the earlier policy crashes. This will finally test whether local winner-take-all competition can beat other non-gated support selectors on anchor-quality rather than being discarded for an execution-layer mismatch.

`group_topk` still has no valid quality signal because its first actual proxy run timed out at the first step, which is stronger evidence of an implementation bottleneck than of a bad architecture. The next coherent move is a performance-focused code pass that makes the `group_topk` encoder path more execution-friendly so the family can receive one clean proxy measurement at K=128 under the strongest current regime.

In crash recovery mode, the safest informative move is to return to the current best working gated K=128 configuration and make the smallest remaining stabilization adjustment. Setting AUXK_ALPHA from 0.01 to 0 tests whether the new gated anchor is still slightly over-regularized while keeping architecture, K, expansion factor, optimizer, and learning rate fixed.

topk.design_hypothesis: Five non-improving rounds on gated-specific tweaks suggest the current search direction is locally exhausted, so the next meaningful move is to revisit a fundamentally different sparse selection mechanism. A clean top-k mainline comparison at K=128 using the current strongest training regime may show whether gated-specific routing is the bottleneck rather than the optimizer or regularization settings.
next_steps: If top-k proxy FVU improves materially over its earlier result and is competitive with gated, promote it to full and begin top-k stabilization from this stronger anchor.
If top-k remains clearly worse even under the stronger regime, conclude that a different architectural direction is needed and retire top-k from near-term focus.
If top-k is competitive on quality but notably better on runtime or stability, keep it alive as an alternate mainline family for subsequent cost-quality tradeoff work.

recent_performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)

round 39: `group_topk` still has no valid quality signal because its first actual proxy run timed out at the first step, which is stronger evidence of an implementation bottleneck than of a bad architecture. The next coherent move is a performance-focused code pass that makes the `group_topk` encoder path more execution-friendly so the family can receive one clean proxy measurement at K=128 under the strongest current regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=sanity_failed)
next_move_guidance: If AUXK_ALPHA=0 improves proxy FVU, promote it to full and treat auxk-free gated training as the new stabilization candidate.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0 is worse but training remains healthy, restore AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 and test a nearby small nonzero value only if policy still requires stability-first tuning.
If AUXK removal degrades quality clearly, keep AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 as the gated default and resume broader exploration only after crash recovery ends.
